# komodo_build (Resource)

Manages a Komodo build resource. Builds define how a Docker image is built from source and pushed to a registry.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "komodo_build" "example" {
  name   = "my-app"
  repo   = "myorg/my-app"
  branch = "main"

  image_name         = "my-app"
  include_latest_tag = true
  auto_increment_version = true

  build_path      = "."
  dockerfile_path = "Dockerfile"

  image_registry {
    domain  = "docker.io"
    account = "myregistry"
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

- `name` (Required) - The unique name of the build. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `builder_id` (Optional) - The ID of the builder to use. Leave empty to use the default builder.
- `version` (Optional) - Semantic version block for the built image. Managed automatically when `auto_increment_version` is enabled.
  - `major` (Optional) - Major version component.
  - `minor` (Optional) - Minor version component.
  - `patch` (Optional) - Patch version component.
- `auto_increment_version` (Optional) - Whether to automatically increment the patch version on each build. Defaults to `true`.
- `image_name` (Optional) - Override for the image name. Defaults to the build name.
- `image_tag` (Optional) - An extra tag suffix to apply to the image.
- `include_latest_tag` (Optional) - Whether to push a `:latest` tag alongside the versioned tag.
- `include_version_tags` (Optional) - Whether to push individual semver component tags (e.g. `:1`, `:1.2`).
- `include_commit_tag` (Optional) - Whether to push a tag with the git commit hash.
- `links` (Optional) - Quick links associated with this build (list of strings).
- `linked_repo` (Optional) - The name of a Komodo Repo resource to link for source code.
- `git_provider` (Optional) - Git provider domain, e.g. `github.com`. Defaults to `github.com`.
- `git_https` (Optional) - Whether to use HTTPS for git access. Defaults to `true`.
- `git_account` (Optional) - Git account to use for private repositories.
- `repo` (Optional) - Repository path, e.g. `owner/repo`.
- `branch` (Optional) - Branch to build from.
- `commit` (Optional) - Specific commit hash to build. Overrides `branch`.
- `webhook_enabled` (Optional) - Whether to allow triggering this build via webhook.
- `webhook_secret` (Optional, Sensitive) - Override the default webhook secret for this build.
- `files_on_host` (Optional) - Whether to use files on the host filesystem for the build context instead of a git repository.
- `build_path` (Optional) - Path to the Docker build context directory. Defaults to `.`.
- `dockerfile_path` (Optional) - Path to the Dockerfile relative to `build_path`.
- `image_registry` (Optional) - List of image registry configurations to push the built image to.
  - `domain` (Optional) - Registry provider domain, e.g. `docker.io`.
  - `account` (Optional) - Account to use with this registry.
  - `organization` (Optional) - Optional organization name within the registry account.
- `skip_secret_interp` (Optional) - Whether to skip secret interpolation in build args.
- `use_buildx` (Optional) - Whether to use `docker buildx` for multi-platform builds.
- `extra_args` (Optional) - Additional arguments to pass to the `docker build` command (list of strings).
- `pre_build` (Optional) - A command to run before the Docker build.
  - `path` (Optional) - The working directory for the command.
  - `command` (Optional) - The shell command to run.
- `dockerfile` (Optional) - Inline Dockerfile contents. Overrides `dockerfile_path` when set.
- `build_args` (Optional) - Docker build arguments in `KEY=VALUE` format, newline-separated.
- `secret_args` (Optional, Sensitive) - Docker secret build arguments. These are not stored in the image layers.
- `labels` (Optional) - Docker image labels in `KEY=VALUE` format, newline-separated.

## Attribute Reference

In addition to the above arguments, the following computed attributes are exported:

- `id` - The build identifier (ObjectId).

## Import

Builds can be imported using the build ID:

```shell
terraform import komodo_build.example <build-id>
```
